alerts.title: Iran declares full Strait of Hormuz blockade; Houthis enforce Red Sea closure
alerts.summary: Iran announced complete closure of Strait of Hormuz to all shipping connected to US-Israel coalition, with Houthis simultaneously blocking Bab el-Mandeb strait. Rybar (147K views, June 8) confirms Iran declared full blockade canceling all prior transit exceptions. Combined closures threaten ~30% of global oil transit and trigger sharp Brent crude spike to $90.97/bbl. Houthis declared total ban on Israeli shipping in Red Sea per incidents feed.
alerts.region: Strait of Hormuz, Red Sea, Persian Gulf

alerts.title: Iran nuclear enrichment reaches 60% amid escalated IAEA pressure campaign
alerts.summary: Iran's uranium enrichment confirmed at 60% U-235 with breakout window narrowing to 1-2 weeks, per global_metrics nuclear feed. US draft resolution at IAEA demands Iran open bombed sites and restart cooperation. Five concurrent nuclear headlines (Reuters, Jerusalem Post, Al Jazeera) indicate coordinated Western pressure campaign intensifying concurrent with missile exchange.

alerts.title: Iran and Israel exchange direct missile strikes; fragile ceasefire holds with Trump intervention
alerts.summary: Iran initiated barrage of missiles at Israel on June 8; Israel responded with airstrikes on Iranian military targets same day. Multiple tier-1 outlets (Reuters, NYT, WSJ, BBC) confirm both sides declared halt to strikes 'for now.' Trump warned Netanyahu against major retaliation, reportedly threatening to leave Israel 'on its own' if escalation continued. Ceasefire remains fragile and vulnerable to proxy action.

alerts.title: Tehran-linked cyber unit claims widespread Israeli radar penetration attack
alerts.summary: Iranian cyber operators asserted 'widespread' penetration of Israeli radar systems per Shafaq News (cyber_headlines feed). Report appears coordinated with kinetic missile exchange, suggesting hybrid warfare escalation. Concurrent with broader CSIS analysis on how Middle East conflict reshapes global cyber landscape, indicating structural shift in conflict methodology.

alerts.title: US maintains three-carrier combat posture amid nuclear and maritime threats
alerts.summary: Assets feed confirms USS Eisenhower (Red Sea), USS Abraham Lincoln (Arabian Sea), and USS Gerald R. Ford (Mediterranean) remain deployed alongside USS Florida submarine in Persian Gulf (26.20°N, 51.56°E). Triple-carrier presence designed to deter Iranian escalation and protect oil chokepoint. Concurrent with Houthi and Iranian blockade threats to regional shipping.
alerts.region: Persian Gulf, Red Sea, Arabian Sea

analysis_note: Iran-Israel direct exchange and dual maritime blockade declarations signal shift from proxy warfare to state-level confrontation; nuclear enrichment at 60% and 1-2 week breakout window create structural time pressure for Western intervention or negotiated settlement within days.
